summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a stellar Colorado run with smoking Dark Star, Iko Iko and a monster Deal.
review: The Miller SBD source is consistently praised as crystal‑clear and the best‑sounding mix of this era, letting listeners hear every nuance of the band’s tight first set and the six‑song pre‑drums stretch.  The show shines on Loose Lucy, Deal, Iko Iko, Dark Star → Terrapin Station and a rousing encore with The Weight, delivering one of the most memorable Colorado performances of 1990.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers award 4–5 stars and highlight both excellent sound quality and exceptional musical moments; only one low‑rating comment is unrelated to performance.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the Miller SBD mix "best sound quality" and note its clarity, with no significant dropouts or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight first set leads into a six‑song pre‑drums sequence (China > Rider > Little Light Rain > Dark Star > Iko Iko > Terrapin) that builds momentum, followed by a solid post‑drums second half and an uplifting encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring lead guitar tone and trippy noodling on Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Keys: Hornsby’s volume described as "just right" and his playing contributes to the overall tightness of the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums segment called a "delight"; post‑drums portion noted as well‑played though more formulaic.
